Shock (1977) 👁️🕯️
📽️ Genre: Horror • Supernatural • Psychological
🎬 Director: Mario Bava
🖊️ Writers: Mario Bava, Dardano Sacchetti, Alfredo Leone
⭐ Cast: Daria Nicolodi (Dora Baldini), John Steiner (Bruno Baldini), David Colin Jr. (Marco)
🕒 Runtime: 92 minutes
🌎 Language: Italian
📅 Release Date: 1977
🎞️ Country: Italy
📖 Plot
Shock follows Dora, a woman recovering from a mental breakdown who returns to her old home with her husband and young son. Soon, strange and terrifying events begin to unfold. Dora becomes convinced that the spirit of her deceased first husband may be tormenting her—and possibly possessing her child. As reality and hallucination blur, the house turns into a psychological prison where grief, guilt, and supernatural forces collide.
✨ Why Watch?
🧠 Pure psychological horror — Less about gore, more about mental unraveling.
👻 Classic haunted-house vibes — Claustrophobic, eerie, and deeply unsettling.
🎥 Mario Bava’s final film — A master filmmaker’s last statement on fear.
🎭 Daria Nicolodi shines — A raw, emotionally intense performance.
🕯️ Atmosphere over everything — Sound design, shadows, and silence do the work.
📊 Reception
🍅 Rotten Tomatoes: Generally positive among horror critics
⭐ Audience Response: Cult appreciation, especially among fans of Italian horror
🎭 Status: Underrated gem of 1970s psychological horror
💡 Fun Facts
🎬 Shock is considered Mario Bava’s final directorial work before his death in 1980.
🎵 The haunting score was composed by Libra, enhancing the film’s surreal tension.
📼 The movie gained popularity later through home video releases under alternate titles like Beyond the Door II.
👁️ Influenced later psychological horror films that focus on maternal fear and possession.
💬 Fan Reactions
“Quiet, cruel, and deeply disturbing.”
“A slow descent into madness done right.”

70S AESTHETIC. There’s no vibe vibier than the 70s. If you aren’t obsessing over 70s aesthetic, it’s time to get into it. The colors (browns, greens, mustards), the texture (wood, shag, leather, brick, plants), the layouts (conversation pits, open floorpans, but with doorway separations) were all designed around entertaining — it’s all gorgeous and it’s all coming back. The cold feeling of the white walls and beige furniture are turning once again and they are heading toward the warmth of the 70s — and I’m here for it.

"Naquela Mesa", eternizada na voz de Nelson Gonçalves, é uma das canções mais emocionantes da música brasileira. Composta por Sérgio Bittencourt em 1972 como uma homenagem póstuma a seu pai, o grande Jacob do Bandolim, a música carrega uma forte carga sentimental, abordando a saudade e a ausência de alguém querido. A letra nostálgica descreve lembranças de conversas e momentos vividos ao redor de uma mesa, agora vazia, intensificando o sentimento de perda. Na interpretação de Nelson Gonçalves, com sua voz marcante e emotiva, Naquela Mesa se tornou um clássico da música brasileira, tocando gerações com sua melancolia e profundidade.

the culture of the 1970s was defined by freedom, experimentation, and change. music played a huge role, with disco, rock, and funk dominating the scene, while artists like the rolling stones, fleetwood mac, and the bee gees shaped the decade’s sound. fashion was bold and expressive, featuring bell-bottoms, platform shoes, and bright patterns that reflected the carefree spirit of the time. the decade also saw the rise of feminism, environmentalism, and civil rights movements that challenged traditional norms. television and film became powerful reflections of society, exploring new ideas and breaking taboos. overall, the 1970s was a time of individuality, creativity, and social transformation.

Кэжуал (Casuals) — это субкультура футбольных фанатов, которая возникла в Англии в 1970-х годах. Она ассоциируется с футбольным хулиганством, дорогими дизайнерскими брендами одежды (такими как Stone Island, Burberry, C.P. Company) и спортивным стилем. Стиль одежды, изначально служивший для идентификации, позже стал частью массовой моды. 
Истоки: Субкультура возникла в Англии в 70-х годах среди футбольных фанатов, которые носили дорогую одежду, чтобы выделиться и выглядеть респектабельно, не привлекая внимания полицейских.
Характерные черты: Помимо футбольного хулиганства, ключевой особенностью была приверженность к дизайнерской одежде. Это включало рубашки поло (особенно Lacoste), кроссовки (Adidas, Diadora), джинсы (Lee Cooper) и куртки-парки.
Эволюция: В 90-х годах стиль вышел за пределы фанатской среды и начал развиваться в высокой моде, например, через коллекции Нино Черрути и Джорджио Армани. В итоге, он трансформировался в более широкое понятие "повседневного" или "удобного" стиля одежды, который используется по всему миру. #парнираньше #парнисейчас #парни #раньшебылолучше #романтика #времена #популярное #fyp #рек #щп

Lançada em 1975, “Lovin’ You” ficou marcada pela delicadeza da melodia e, principalmente, pela voz celestial de Minnie Riperton famosa por atingir notas tão agudas que beiram o impossível. O que muitos não sabem é que essa música foi escrita por ela ao lado do marido, Richard Rudolph, e dedicada à filha do casal: Maya Rudolph, que anos depois se tornaria uma comediante conhecida em Hollywood.

Com uma atmosfera quase mágica, a faixa fala sobre um amor calmo e verdadeiro, acompanhada por arranjos suaves e o som real de passarinhos ao fundo.

E sim… esse som de pássaros foi mesmo gravado no quintal da casa de Minnie, e a gravação vocal foi feita de primeira, em apenas uma tomada.

Um dos registros vocais mais impressionantes e emocionantes da história da música.

Fonte: site Wikipédia, site Genius

"Cherry Bomb", the explosive 1976 debut single by The Runaways, takes on even greater power in this live performance. Written by Joan Jett and Kim Fowley, the song became an anthem of teenage rebellion and female empowerment. On stage, with Cherie Currie’s commanding vocals and the band’s raw energy, “Cherry Bomb” captures the essence of rock ‘n’ roll attitude — loud, fearless, and unapologetic. This performance remains a defining moment in the legacy of women in rock.

This WILD “Sweet Fish” commercial from the 70s is pure unhinged energy and we can’t look away 😭
 
For context: This was a PARODY ad mocking how aggressively brands pushed feminine hygiene products back in the day. The tagline literally said women needed to smell like “sweet fish” instead of... well, you know 🫠
 
Wild Facts:
- This fake commercial became more famous than REAL ads from that era
- It was designed to expose how sexist beauty standards were literally telling women their bodies were “wrong”
- The ad circulated for DECADES before people realized it was satire
- It’s now a cultural time capsule of outdated marketing insanity
 
The 70s were a different time, y’all. Thank goodness we’ve evolved... mostly 👀
